summary: Added a linked decomp.dev progress image to .github/README.md, displaying the doldecomp/melee project progress at 512x256 near the top of the README. This was a documentation/presentation-only change with no code or decomp matching impact.
what_changed: Inserted an HTML image tag into .github/README.md immediately below the project heading and Discord badge section.
The image source points to https://decomp.dev/doldecomp/melee.svg?w=512&h=256 and is rendered with width="512" and height="256".
The image is wrapped in a Markdown reference link targeting [Progress], relying on an existing or elsewhere-defined README link reference.
smart_moves: Used decomp.dev as the source of truth for a visual project progress graphic instead of committing a static generated image.
Specified fixed image dimensions in the README to keep layout predictable.
Placed the progress image prominently near existing status badges so repository visitors can quickly assess project status.
decomp_lessons: Decompilation progress visibility can be improved through README metadata without touching source, build scripts, or matching-sensitive files.
For project-facing documentation, dynamic decomp.dev assets are useful for communicating current progress while avoiding manual image updates.
This PR provides no evidence about matching tactics, symbol naming, struct layout, or code organization because it only changed README content.
naming_conventions: Retained existing Markdown reference-link style, using a capitalized reference label [Progress] for the linked image.
No C symbol, function, struct, enum, or header naming conventions were introduced or modified.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: None; the diff only updates .github/README.md and contains no source or assembly changes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find README changes that add or update decomp.dev progress badges or images.
Find documentation-only PRs that improve project status visibility.
Check whether [Progress] link reference is defined in .github/README.md after this change.
